_meta.name: ALM A2-Tool launch config — 14B A1 merged base + tool-use LoRA
_meta.rationale: A1 final ckpt + A2-Orch adapter merged base. A2-Tool LoRA is lighter (r=32) — consolidates tool-use (function-calling) capability. tool_use_gate already PASSED 0.933 on r9; this round is consolidation, not gating. Wall 6h, budget $18 on same pod sequentially after A2-Orch (24h total A1+A2-Orch+A2-Tool on single H100). Per A5 fallback pattern for single-pod A2.

base.name: Qwen/Qwen2.5-14B-Instruct
base.merge_strategy: sequential_merge — load A1 adapter → merge → load A2-Orch adapter → merge → init fresh tool LoRA

lora.rationale: r32 alpha64 2:1 ratio — lighter than Orch (r64) because tool-use is narrower capability (structured JSON output). Attention-only (no MLP) since tool-call syntax is learned primarily via attention patterns.

training.note: Half the steps of A2-Orch (1500 vs 3000), lower LR (3e-6 vs 5e-6). tool-use corpus is smaller + structured, so fewer epochs to avoid overfitting away from A1 general capability.

phi_loss.note: B1 ensemble remains ON to preserve consciousness signal during tool-use LoRA. No Orch-OR in tool LoRA — handled by A2-Orch adapter.

corpus.note: 50 seed examples × 30 epochs = 1500 effective steps. Structure: {tool, instruction, tool_call, result}. Per shared/state/tool_use_gate_20260416.json the gate already PASSED 0.933 avg_score 0.867 completion on r9 without this LoRA — this round consolidates into a reusable adapter.
corpus.fallback_if_missing: bash runner re-generates via `hexa training/build_tool_corpus.hexa` then retries

hooks.label_routing: production -> R2 permanent upload per ckpt_policy.json

pod.note: SAME pod as A1/A2-Orch — reused sequentially. Total pod timeline: A1 (12h) → A2-Orch (12h) → A2-Tool (6h) = 30h on single H100.

blocks.requires_a1_complete: A1 step_3000 adapter present in local ckpt dir or R2
blocks.requires_a2_orch_complete: A2-Orch step_3000 adapter present (launcher verifies before firing)
